headline: Witness protection programme shelters only 89 witnesses since 2009
context: Malaysia's Witness Protection Act 2009 has sheltered only 89 witnesses in 16 years, raising questions about its effectiveness in combating serious crime.

cards.big: Only 89 witnesses protected under 2009 Act in 16 years of operation
cards.sub: Low uptake suggests programme fails to inspire confidence in potential witnesses

cards.big: Witness Protection Act 2009 allows relocation and identity change for witnesses
cards.sub: Attorney General's consent required before any protection can be offered

cards.big: Right to security of person under Article 5 extends to those assisting justice
cards.sub: Without effective protection, witnesses in organised crime cases face retaliation

cards.big: Prosecution success rate in organised crime cases dropped to 34% in 2024
cards.sub: Witness intimidation cited as factor in 45% of discontinued prosecutions

cards.big: 89 protected witnesses in 16 years means the justice system cannot protect its own process
MACC proposed independent witness protection unit separate from police in 2024
